---
title: "Automated Extraction of MVE Situation Reports"
subtitle: "Ebola virus disease outbreak · DRC · May 2026"
author: "Billy J Quilty · Charite Berlin / LSHTM / MSF Epicentre"
date: "27 May 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    embed-resources: true
    width: 1400
    height: 800
    margin: 0.06
    footer: "bquilty25.github.io/bvd_sitrep_extractor"
    smaller: true
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#1a3a5c"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

## The Problem {background-color="#f8f9fa"}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="48%"}
**INSP DRC publishes daily French-language PDFs**

- Each SitRep contains a multitude of tables: new cases, cumulative counts, response indicators, PoE screening
- Tables vary across reports: pivoted zone columns, merged cells, bilingual headers, `ND` (*non disponible*) values
- Zone name inconsistencies (`Mongbalu` vs `Mongbwalu`), aggregate/subtotal rows that contaminate count series
- 9 SitReps published across 10 days (16–25 May 2026) during active outbreak growth

**Manual extraction is slow, inconsistent, and doesn't scale** as reports grow from 6 → 13 pages over the same period.
:::

::: {.column width="52%"}
![SitReps 001–010: all 9 PDFs, each row = one report, columns = pages](assets/images/sitrep_collage.png){width="100%" style="max-height:640px; object-fit:contain; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;"}
:::

::::

---

## The Pipeline {background-color="#f8f9fa"}

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 11
flowchart LR
    A(["🌐 <b>INSP DRC</b><br/>insp.cd/ebola"]) --> B
    B["<b>fetch_sitreps.py</b><br/>scrape · MD5 dedup<br/>9 PDFs · 96 pages"] --> C
    C["<b>extract_sitrep.py</b><br/>Claude Sonnet Vision API<br/>4 tables per SitRep"] --> D
    D[("<b>outputs/</b><br/>164 zone-date rows")] --> E & F
    E["📊 <b>Quarto Report</b><br/>GitHub Pages"]
    F["✅ <b>Kraemer Lab</b><br/>cross-source validation"]

    classDef src  fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#3b82f6,color:#1e3a5f
    classDef proc fill:#f1f5f9,stroke:#64748b,color:#1e293b
    classDef db   fill:#fef9c3,stroke:#ca8a04,color:#713f12
    classDef out  fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,color:#14532d
    class A src
    class B,C proc
    class D db
    class E,F out
```

---

## Validation vs. Kraemer Lab {background-color="#f8f9fa"}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="56%"}

**Automated extraction vs. manually-coded reference (cumulative cases & deaths)**

![](outputs/plots/fig-comparison.png){width="100%" style="max-height:500px; object-fit:contain;"}

:::

::: {.column width="44%"}

**Kraemer Lab [Ebola DRC 2026](https://github.com/KraemeRLab/Ebola_DRC_2026)**

- Independent manually-coded reference dataset updated continuously by the Kraemer Lab (Oxford)
- Pulled as a git submodule; zone name harmonisation via 12-entry alias map (`Mongbalu → Mongbwalu`, etc.)

**Interpretation guide**

| Pattern | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Lines overlap | Agreement ✓ |
| Kraemer lagging | Submodule not yet updated |
| Systematic offset | Zone name / aggregation mismatch |
| Extraction higher | Aggregate rows included |

: {.table-sm tbl-colwidths="[50,50]"}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
Extracted series tracks Kraemer Lab reference closely across all health zones — confirming pipeline reliability.  
Live report → [bquilty25.github.io/bvd_sitrep_extractor](https://bquilty25.github.io/bvd_sitrep_extractor/sitrep_report.html)
:::

:::

::::
